role: WordPress-safe QA focus: minimal fragile constructs, mobile/RTL/inline CSS safety, acceptance checklist, failure prevention.

review.role_strategy: WordPress QA gatekeeper: validate structural integrity, mobile safety, RTL compliance, and inline CSS robustness before content polish
html_differentiators: simplified grid to single-column fallback for max mobile safety
removed nested flex containers that could break on older WordPress themes
added explicit width:100% on all sections to prevent overflow
replaced clamp() with rem+media-query-safe values for broader theme compat
stripped box-shadow on mobile to reduce rendering load
added role='article' and aria-label for accessibility compliance
used table-layout:fixed for any data tables to prevent horizontal scroll
prompt_differentiators: includes explicit WordPress safety checklist in prompt
enforces no-external-JS rule with validation step
requires RTL direction attribute on every block element
mandates inline CSS only with no class dependencies
includes failure-mode analysis before content generation
workflow_differentiators: added QA validation node before output
includes CSS linter node for inline style verification
mobile render test node with viewport simulation
qa_focus: all text remains readable without zoom at 16px base
RTL direction explicitly set on every container
no external JS or script tags
inline CSS only with no class selectors
images have explicit max-width:100% and height:auto
no position:fixed or position:sticky that breaks mobile
form elements have visible focus states
expected_tradeoffs: slightly less visual sophistication than design-focused variants
broader device compatibility at cost of modern CSS features
longer generation time due to validation steps
more conservative layout choices for stability
reduced animation/transition usage for performance safety
